gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/172)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/172)
text: قَالَ يَا حُسَيْنُ أَنْفِقْ وَ أَيْقِنْ بِالْخَلَفِ مِنَ اللَّهِ فَإِنَّهُ لَمْ يَبْخَلْ عَبْدٌ وَ لَا أَمَةٌ بِنَفَقَةٍ فِيمَا يُرْضِي اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِلَّا أَنْفَقَ أَضْعَافَهَا فِيمَا يُسْخِطُ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from his father, from Sa’dan, from Al Husayn Bin Ayman,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘O Husayn! Spend and have certainty in the Replacement from Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, for neither will a slave nor a maid be stingy with expenditure regarding what Pleases Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic, except that they would end up spending in what Angers Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic’.
en.sarwar: 6. Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid has narrated from Jahm ibn al-Hakam al-Mad’iniy from Isma‘il ibn abu Ziyad who has said the following: “Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, has said, There are three kinds of hands: the asking hand, the withholding hand, and the spending hand, of which the best hand is the spending hand.’”
